es.meaning: Once in a blue moon; very rarely.
es.example: Nos vemos cada muerte de obispo.
es.exampleTranslation: We see each other once in a blue moon.
es.regionNote: LatAm form; Spain also knows 'de Pascuas a Ramos' and 'de uvas a peras'. Reviewer to confirm MX currency.

it.meaning: Once in a blue moon; very rarely.
it.example: Ci sentiamo ogni morte di papa.
it.exampleTranslation: We talk once in a blue moon.

bridge.note: The same joke told at different ranks: rarity measured in clerical funerals — Italy waits for a pope to die, Latin America only for a bishop. The hierarchy gap is the delight: same Church, same morbid clock, calibrated to local ecclesiastical supply.

interference.es_to_it: Promoting the cleric — 'ogni morte di vescovo' — gets a laugh, not the idiom. In Italy only the pope's death keeps time.
interference.it_to_es: Demoting works no better: 'cada muerte de papa' is understood and marked. The Spanish clock runs on bishops.

review.notes: Discovered via FluentU list (Joe, 2026-07-16); all content authored fresh, ES pairing by Claude — verify. Hook candidate: 'Italy waits for a pope to die. Mexico only needs a bishop.'
